title: Lamyra tauricola
taxonomy: Lamyra tauricola is classified as a species of dragonfly within the family Aeshnidae. This classification places Lamyra tauricola within the order Odonata, which comprises all dragonflies and damselflies. The species is further categorized into the genus Lamyra, which consists of several species of dragonflies. The genus Lamyra is part of the subfamily Brachydiplacinae, a group of dragonflies characterized by their distinctive wing patterns and habits. Lamyra tauricola is specifically identified as a subspecies of Lamyra maculata.
constituents: Lamyra tauricola is a species of spider, not a plant. However, I can tell you about the general active constituents found in spiders, but not specifically Lamyra tauricola. intro: Lamyra tauricola, also known as Tauricola kochiana or Wild Marjoram, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The health benefits of Lamyra tauricola include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Lamyra tauricola is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to create medicinal remedies for various health conditions.
